questions.text: In general, I feel satisfied with my life.
I feel a sense of meaning and purpose in what I do each day.
How often do you feel genuinely joyful or "in good spirits"?
I feel connected to the people I care about.
When something goes wrong, I'm usually able to bounce back fairly quickly.
I feel engaged and energized by the things I do in a typical week.
How often do you feel calm and at ease rather than anxious or overwhelmed?
I feel a sense of accomplishment from things I've done recently.
On a typical week, how much of it actually feels good to you?
Overall, if 1 is "really struggling" and 5 is "truly thriving," where would you put yourself right now?
I believe I can actively train my mind to be happier — it's a skill, not just luck.
When a negative thought shows up, I'm able to reframe it into something more useful.
I have a daily routine or practice that strengthens my mindset.
I understand how my brain forms habits and can use that to my advantage.
I notice my self-talk and can shift it when it turns against me.
I take deliberate action to improve my mood rather than waiting for it to pass.
I can stay focused on what matters even when I'm under pressure.
I believe my effort directly shapes how my life turns out.
When I set an intention for the day, I'm able to follow through on it.
I treat setbacks as information I can learn from, not as proof of failure.
I regularly notice and appreciate the good things in my life.
I have a clear sense of what I'm working toward and why it matters.
When things get hard, I can zoom out and keep the bigger picture in view.
I feel my daily actions are connected to something larger than myself.
I wake up with a sense of optimism about the day ahead.

tiers.label: Aware
tiers.description: You see the levers but don't pull them yet.

tiers.label: Activated
tiers.description: You're using the methodology in real life.

tiers.label: Mastery
tiers.description: You've made it your operating system.
